content:
Case 17:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 44
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Scheduled follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL, with interest in outpatient treatment options
- Vital signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP not reported at this visit, HR not reported at this visit, RR not reported at this visit, SpO₂ not reported at this visit.
Setting: Outpatient hematology/oncology follow-up after alloSCT and CAR-T
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in a routine follow-up setting after intensive leukemia therapy. You can review prior history and recent clinical course, perform a targeted examination, and request standard follow-up evaluations appropriate to an ambulatory visit. Decisions focus on surveillance and whether further workup for relapse or complications is indicated.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

solve.working_diagnosis: Post-treatment follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL

treatment_plan.action: Review recent laboratory results and imaging studies
Perform a physical examination focusing on signs of relapse, GVHD, or infection
Assess patient's current symptoms and overall well-being
Discuss and educate the patient on signs and symptoms of relapse, GVHD, and infection
Schedule regular follow-up visits and necessary laboratory tests

content:
Round 2 begins.
Setting: Hematology follow-up with bone marrow reassessment
Capabilities: The patient is undergoing more detailed evaluation because of his leukemia history and evolving laboratory findings. You can organize marrow-based assessments and additional consultations to clarify disease status. Some specialized tests are still pending.
Request additional information or solve.

incorrect

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the results of the bone marrow reassessment and any pending specialized tests."}}

response.request: Please provide the results of the bone marrow reassessment and any pending specialized tests.

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow biopsy (2024-07-09): The marrow was hypocellular (cellularity 25%) with therapy-related toxic changes. Trilineage hematopoiesis was present but displayed dysplastic features: megakaryopoiesis was reduced with small, hypolobulated forms; erythropoiesis showed megaloblastoid maturation; and granulopoiesis exhibited toxic granulation and nuclear irregularities. There was no blast excess (<5% by morphology).
Flow cytometry revealed a normal distribution of myeloid and lymphoid populations with T-cells showing mild inversion of the CD4/CD8 ratio. A small blast population (0.1%) was detected, characterized by CD19-/CD22-, CD34^dim, CD33-, CD10-, CD20-, CD117+, CD5+, CD7+, CD56-, cyMPO-, cyTdT-, cyCD3-.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Bone marrow hypocellularity with dysplastic features post alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy", "differentials": ["Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS)", "Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm", "Immune-mediated marrow suppression"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Monitor complete blood count (CBC) and peripheral blood smear regularly", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy and flow cytometry in 3 months or earlier if there are significant changes in CBC or new symptoms", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consult with a hematopathologist to review bone marrow findings and assess for any additional specialized testing", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Provide supportive care and monitor for signs of infection, bleeding, or other complications", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Consider referral to a hematologist with expertise in myelodysplastic syndromes and therapy-related myeloid neoplasms for further evaluation and management", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Bone marrow hypocellularity with dysplastic features post alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy

treatment_plan.action: Monitor complete blood count (CBC) and peripheral blood smear regularly
Repeat bone marrow biopsy and flow cytometry in 3 months or earlier if there are significant changes in CBC or new symptoms
Consult with a hematopathologist to review bone marrow findings and assess for any additional specialized testing
Provide supportive care and monitor for signs of infection, bleeding, or other complications
Consider referral to a hematologist with expertise in myelodysplastic syndromes and therapy-related myeloid neoplasms for further evaluation and management

content:
Round 3 begins.
Setting: Relapse and transplant planning conference in a heavily pretreated leukemia patient
Capabilities: You now have results from bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, clonality assays, and broad molecular profiling together with a comprehensive treatment history including alloSCT, bispecifics, inotuzumab, blinatumomab, and CD19 CAR-T. You are expected to interpret the pattern of MRD relapse, phenotypic evolution, and donor-derived CHIP, and to formulate a realistic outpatient-oriented salvage and transplant-bridging strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

solve.working_diagnosis: Minimal residual disease (MRD) relapse of B-ALL with phenotypic evolution and donor-derived clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) post alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy

treatment_plan.action: Initiate outpatient salvage therapy with a targeted agent such as a bispecific antibody or antibody-drug conjugate
Monitor response to salvage therapy with regular peripheral blood MRD assessments and bone marrow evaluations
Consult with a transplant specialist to evaluate the feasibility of a second alloSCT or other cellular therapies
Provide supportive care and monitor for signs of infection, GVHD, or other complications
Consider enrollment in a clinical trial for novel therapies or combinations, if available and appropriate
